_meta.method: Cross-referenced the Sōtōshū South America directory (sotozen.com/eng/temples/outside_jp/Brazil), Comunidade Zen Budista Zendo Brasil (Monja Coen sangha), Zendo Curitiba (sotozen.com.br affiliated), Comunidade Daissen network, Mosteiro Zen Morro da Vargem and Mosteiro Eishōji (Tokuda lineage), Templo Zen das Alterosas, Templo Enkoji, Templo Taikanji, Via Zen Porto Alegre, plus Plum Village Brasil sangha index curated by A Outra Margem and the Sangavirtual blog. Each entry verified against an official lineage organisation, the centre's own site, or both.
_meta.coverageEstimate: High (~75-80%) for Sōtōshū-registered temples and the largest active sanghas (Coen, Daissen, Tokuda, Morro da Vargem, AZI). Plum Village Brasil sangha network undercounted (many are informal weekly Zoom/home groups without standalone sites). Limited or no presence located for Rinzai (other than Tokuda's Rinzai-influenced lineage), Kwan Um, Sanbō Zen, Ōbaku.
knownGaps: Plum Village informal home sanghas (Sangha Plena Consciência São Paulo, Sangha Viver Consciente Rio, Wake Up São Paulo, Sangha Zen Despertar SC) lack standalone websites — only the Pasargada Ecovillage hub is fully sourced
Daissen sub-locations (Salvador, Recife) are referenced but specific street addresses not published on daissen.org.br
Centro Zen do Recife / Seirenji (Tokuda) has only a blogspot presence and uncertain current activity
Templo Zen Pico de Raios (Ouro Preto) and Mosteiro Serra do Trovão (Lavras Novas) appear active but rely on blog-era sourcing
No active Rinzai-only, Kwan Um, Sanbō Zen, or Ōbaku centres located as of 2026

places.name: Comunidade Budista Sōtō Zenshū da América do Sul — Busshinji
places.address: Rua São Joaquim 285, Liberdade, São Paulo - SP
places.notes: Founded 1956. Official headquarters of Sōtōshū for South America. Hosts the Sul-Americano regional office.

places.name: Comunidade Zen Budista Zendo Brasil — Taikōzan Tenzuizenji
places.address: Rua Wanderley 1302, Perdizes, São Paulo - SP, 05018-001
places.notes: Founded by Monja Coen Rōshi. Sōtōshū-registered. Building a permanent temple in Campos do Jordão.

places.name: Daissen-ji — Comunidade Zen Budista de Florianópolis
places.address: Travessa José Goulart 59, Saco dos Limões, Florianópolis - SC
places.notes: Main seat of the Daissen Zen-Buddhist community network. Linktree: linktr.ee/zendaissen.

places.name: Dōjō Daiji-dō
places.address: Rua Oscar Vidal 71/301, Centro, Juiz de Fora - MG

places.name: Mosteiro Zen Morro da Vargem — Zenkōji
places.notes: Long-standing residential monastery with environmental and forest-restoration mission.

places.name: Organização Religiosa Budista Zenguenji do Brasil
places.address: Rua Padre Eustáquio 109, Vila Lavínia, Mogi das Cruzes - SP

places.name: Templo Budista de Rolândia — Dōkōzan Busshinji do Brasil
Templo Enkōji
places.address: Rua Izequias Hengles 485, Chácara das Palmeiras, Itapecerica da Serra - SP

places.name: Templo Zen das Alterosas — Kogakuzenji
places.address: Rua Itaparica 245, Serra, Belo Horizonte - MG

places.name: Templo Zen Taikanji
places.address: Estrada das Pintas, km 3,5, Pitangueiras do Meio, Pedra Bela - SP

places.name: Mosteiro Zen Budista Eishōji — Hōryūzan
places.address: Várzea do Lobo, zona rural, Pirenópolis - GO
places.notes: Founded 2001 by Mestre Ryōtan Tokuda Igarashi. 900-hectare ecological sanctuary at 1,200 m altitude.

places.name: Zendo Curitiba
places.lineage: Sōtō (Sōtōshū; Monja Coen lineage — Ryuzan & Mui Sato)
places.notes: Founded 3 September 2013 by Ryuzan and Mui Sato, disciples of Monja Coen Rōshi. Affiliated with Zendo Brasil.

places.name: Via Zen — Centro de Prática Porto Alegre
places.notes: Founded 1996 (roots in 1970s Porto Alegre zazen group). Affiliated with Eiheiji/Sōjiji headquarters. Contact: secretariaviazen@gmail.com.

places.name: Pasargada Ecovillage — Sangha Plum Village Brasil
places.lineage: Vietnamese Thiền (Plum Village / Thich Nhat Hanh)
places.notes: Reference centre for Plum Village tradition in Brazil; sustainability + mindfulness ecovillage.

places.name: Wake Up São Paulo — Plum Village young-adults sangha
places.notes: Wake Up sangha for practitioners aged 18-35 in Plum Village tradition.
